summary: Czech nationalist composer, most commonly identified by the tone poem cycle Ma vlast (which includes The Moldau depicting the Vltava River) and the opera The Bartered Bride. Went deaf in 1874. Wrote the autobiographical string quartet From My Life. Associated with Prague and the Prague Spring Festival. Often clued alongside Dvorak.

works.name: Ma vlast (My Country) — symphonic cycle of 6 tone poems
works.description: His most famous work. Six tone poems depicting Bohemia.

clues.clue: Set of six tone poems depicting his homeland/Bohemia
Prague Spring Festival always opens with a performance of Ma vlast on May 12th
A theme used in the second movement (Vltava) is also used in the Israeli anthem Hatikvah; adapted from the madrigal "La Mantovana"
Dedicated to the city of Prague

works.name: Vltava / The Moldau (from Ma vlast)
works.description: The most famous tone poem from Ma vlast.

clues.clue: Two flutes play swirling/undulating sixteenth note figures to represent the river/waters
Depicts the Vltava river (Moldau in German) flowing past a castle
Theme B, E, F-sharp, G, A, B, B appears after the flute opening
G-minor opening, then fugato in A minor 3/4 time with muted first violins playing descending D6, G-sharp5 tritone
Key changes from one sharp to four flats for "Moonlight: Dance of the Nymphs" episode (flute duet over muted strings)
Appears in the cycle after Vysehrad

works.name: Vysehrad (from Ma vlast)
works.description: The first tone poem in Ma vlast.

clues.clue: Opens with two harps (one strumming, one playing cadenzas) imitating the bard Lumir
Motif B-flat, E-flat, D, B-flat depicting the "High Castle"
Harp solo followed by a brass chorale
Depicts Prague's old castle Vysehrad

works.name: Sarka (from Ma vlast)
works.description: Third tone poem in Ma vlast.

clues.clue: One of the six symphonic poems in Ma vlast

works.name: String Quartet No. 1 in E minor ("From My Life")
works.description: Autobiographical chamber work. Very frequently clued.

clues.clue: Sustained high harmonic E in the first violin represents his deafness/tinnitus in the final movement
Opening viola solo: descending short-long-short motif (eighth note B, whole note B, down to E, eighth F-sharp, whole G, down to B)
Second movement is a polka (viola instructed to play "quasi Tromba" / like a trumpet on the C string)
Third movement opens with exposed cello solo descending from F above the staff to trills below the staff
Dvorak played viola at the private premiere
Letter to friend: number of performers (4) purposefully set to discuss "what so obviously troubles me"
Harsh, quiet tremolos underlie the high E in the final movement
Set of three "Poetic Polkas" for solo piano related to the polka genre
Comic opera; overture opens with rising third A to C and unison strings in F major pentatonic
Dance of the Comedians — accompanies arrival of circus troupe; Vasek dons a bear costume
Character sings the "Stuttering Song"; Esmeralda dances with a bear
Character claims to value love over beer, but betrays lover by accepting 300 florins
Ends with the marriage of Marenka and Jenik
Max Brod translated it into German

works.name: Other Operas (Dalibor, The Brandenburgers in Bohemia)

clues.clue: Dalibor — Milada brings a fiddle to the title character in jail; she falls in love with him after demanding his death; he killed an enemy Burgrave to avenge friend Zdenek
The Brandenburgers in Bohemia — Varneman's warriors fight Habsburgs but are driven out
"Faded Happiness" — first of six Reves, dedicated to a former student in the Thun family
Concert etude On the Seashore
Piano Concerto in G Minor — mourning the death of his daughter Bedriska
Czech nationalist composer
Went deaf in 1874 (full hearing to total deafness over the course of 1874)
Max Brod praised Smetana for mixing nationalism and cosmopolitanism; Brod was his advocate

recursive_suggestions: The Moldau (Vltava) — heavily clued tone poem, likely has its own answerline
From My Life (String Quartet No. 1) — appeared as its own answerline at DECAF 2022
The Bartered Bride — appeared as its own answerline
Vysehrad — the opening tone poem of Ma vlast
